title: Adjectives in the nominative plural
summary: Make adjectives agree with plural masculine animate, masculine inanimate, feminine, and neuter nouns.
objectives: Choose hard-adjective plural endings by gender and animacy
Keep soft adjectives in -í across plural genders
Apply common consonant mutations in masculine animate adjective forms

sections.heading: Masculine inanimate and feminine
sections.body: Hard adjectives use -é with masculine inanimate and feminine plural nouns. Soft adjectives continue to use -í.

sections.heading: Neuter plural
sections.body: Hard adjectives use -á with neuter plural nouns; soft adjectives use -í.

sections.heading: Masculine animate plural
sections.body: Both hard and soft adjectives end in -í. Hard adjectives may mutate their final consonant before this ending.

sections.heading: Common mutations
sections.body: With hard masculine animate plural forms, k changes to c, r to ř, sk to št, and ck to čt.

sections.heading: Plural agreement summary
sections.body: Hard adjectives: masculine animate -í, masculine inanimate and feminine -é, neuter -á. Soft adjectives use -í everywhere.
examples: noví studenti / nové stoly / nové knihy / nová auta
moderní studenti / moderní stoly / moderní knihy / moderní auta

vocabulary.note: Hard adjective, masculine inanimate or feminine plural.
Soft adjective; unchanged across plural genders.

resources.title: Adjectives — nominative plural
resources.note: Plural adjective endings and consonant mutations.

exercises.explanation: Knihy are feminine plural, so a hard adjective uses -é: velké knihy.
Moderní is a soft adjective and keeps -í.

exercises.prompt: Type the masculine animate plural of dobrý.
exercises.explanation: The ending -í triggers r to ř: dobří.

exercises.prompt: Which phrase is correct for Czech teachers?
exercises.explanation: Masculine animate plural uses -í with sk changing to št: čeští.
